mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy rates are falling versus flat or rising.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after monthly declines in FEDFUNDS than after non-declines, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a mild-cycle threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with UNRATE above 4.0% do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if more than 60% of triggered cases are positive over the evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise together, the mechanism is broad nominal demand growth: firms are selling more and hiring more, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-rise condition for RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone in explaining 60-day SPX returns, or if conditional hit rate falls below 55% in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial activity and profit momentum, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not lower after INDPRO downtrends than after flat or rising periods, or if the signal's sign reverses for two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in HOUST above its recent baseline signals a housing-demand rebound that spills into broader cyclical spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakout episodes fail to produce positive mean 20-day SPX returns, or if fewer than 55% of breakout episodes are positive over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of CPIAUCSL remaining elevated while FEDFUNDS rises implies real-rate pressure and a tightening policy reaction, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-plus-hike condition does not beat either proxy alone for 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the conditional average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS accelerates while UNRATE stays low, labor-market momentum supports income growth and risk appetite, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE remains below 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS/UNRATE condition does not yield positive average 60-day returns, or if positive hit rate falls below 60% across triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates weakening real and nominal demand, which should forecast negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS are followed by positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power over single-variable versions.
